body_txt: What if there were someone (or something) that couldn't be seen, even in broad daylight? This is the idea behind the Invisibility trope. Invisibility is such an old trope that there are multiple ways to be invisible, many of which still require a Hand Wave : Some invisible characters can turn their invisibility on and off at will. These characters are often Superheroes . However, invisibility can also be permanent, even if the invisible character falls asleep or dies. Permanent invisibility is usually treated as either Cursed with Awesome or Blessed with Suck depending on the story. These characters have to make do with coping mechanisms if they want other characters to See the Invisible , such as a mask, Body Paint , visible clothes, or a Plot Device such as the DNA Stabilizer in Gemini Man that limits Sam Casey to 15 minutes of invisibility every day. It may be interesting to note that whatever the parameters of the invisibility — permanent or non-permanent, affecting clothes or only the body, voluntary or involuntary, these will usually be taken as the "obvious" properties of invisibility, without any need to explain why they should work according to those rules instead of one of the other permutations. There are a variety of reasons why invisibility appeals to creators : See also Astral Projection , which frequently renders the projected invisible. Compare Wallpaper Camouflage for when it's difficult, but not impossible, to see a character because their clothing looks like the wall behind them, and Weirdness Censor , where characters see something very strange but refuse to believe that they saw it. Not to be confused with He Who Must Not Be Seen . Such a character can't be seen by the audience, usually due to camera angles, but they can presumably be seen by other characters.
